core_concept: The Plant Life Cycle: From Seed to Seed
facts: To germinate, a seed needs water and warmth, but not sunlight; sunlight is needed only after the sprout appears.
After a seed germinates, a tiny root grows downward into the soil first, then a shoot pushes upward toward the light.
A flower is part of the plant that will turn into a fruit; the fruit holds the new seeds that can grow into new plants.
The plant life cycle repeats over and over: a seed grows into a plant, the plant makes flowers and then fruit with seeds, and those seeds can grow into new plants again.
misconceptions: A seed needs sunlight to begin growing into a plant.
Once a flower dies, the plant has finished its life and will never make new seeds.
